diagrams.title: Binary Full Adder Circuit
diagrams.description: A schematic showing a full adder circuit that adds three inputs (A, B, and Carry-in) to produce Sum and Carry-out. This is the fundamental building block for binary addition operations.
elements: AND and OR gates for carry logic
Gate connection lines with bubbles for inversion
diagrams.purpose: Explains how single-bit binary addition works at the hardware level, which is the basis for all arithmetic microoperations in the ALU.

diagrams.title: 4-bit Binary Adder-Subtractor
diagrams.description: A circuit diagram showing four full adders connected in cascade to form a 4-bit parallel adder, with an additional XOR gate on input B to enable subtraction via two's complement (when SUB=1).
elements: A3 A2 A1 A0 inputs (4-bit operand)
B3 B2 B1 B0 inputs (4-bit operand)
XOR gates for B input inversion
S3 S2 S1 S0 sum outputs
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how multi-bit arithmetic operations are performed using chained adders, and how subtraction is implemented using addition of two's complement.

diagrams.title: ALU Arithmetic Section Block Diagram
diagrams.description: A block diagram showing the arithmetic portion of an ALU (Arithmetic Logic Unit) with multiplexers to select between different arithmetic microoperations based on operation select lines.
diagrams.purpose: Shows how the ALU integrates multiple arithmetic microoperations and how the operation is selected, including how status flags are generated to indicate the result condition.
